name: Black Bottom Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix cornstarch and 1/2 cup sugar in a bowl.
2. Gradually add the milk to the egg yolks, then stir into the sugar and cornstarch mixture.
3. Cook over a double boiler, stirring constantly, until the mixture coats a spoon. Remove from heat, stir in vanilla.
4. Set aside 1 cup of this custard and stir in the melted chocolate bits. Pour into the pastry shell and chill.
5. Soften gelatin in cold water and add to the remaining hot custard; stir until dissolved. Add flavoring and chill slightly.
6. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add the remaining sugar, beating until stiff.
7. Fold the custard/gelatin mixture into the egg whites and pile over the chocolate layer in the pie shell. Chill until set.
8. Garnish with chopped walnuts.
Mrs. Henry D. Chaplin
